events.description: Senator James E. Murray introduced S. 716, a bill authorizing issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters, Crow Indian, Wyola, Montana. Bill read twice and referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs.
George Peters wrote a letter to Senator Murray (referenced in Murray's January 26 reply)
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of a patent in fee via the Secretary of the Interior

Department of the Interior (Oscar L. Chapman, Undersecretary) issued report on S. 716 recommending enactment if amended to allow sale to a Crow Indian under existing regulations rather than direct patent in fee

Senator Joseph C. O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and asking if amendments were agreeable
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment, recommending the bill do pass. Report No. 508 committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed.

Senator Murray wrote to George Peters enclosing copy of House report, advising bill passed Senate on April 11 and had been reported favorably out of House Committee on Public Lands, noting it would come up on next private calendar
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senator James E. Murray sent a Day Letter telegram to George Peters, Box 84, Wyola, Montana, advising that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature the previous day

Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee directly
George Peters indicated desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian, in 1921

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters land on Crow Indian Reservation to a Crow Indian under existing regulations, authorized by S. 716

correspondence.subject: Introduction of bill to authorize patent in fee
correspondence.outcome: Peters informed of bill introduction; Murray pledged to do utmost to pass bill at earliest possible date

correspondence.recipient_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Report on S. 716 authorizing patent in fee to George Peters, Crow Indian
correspondence.action_requested: Recommendation to enact bill if amended to allow Secretary to sell land to Crow Indian under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
